title: Can no longer install buildah on ubuntu 20.04 from kubic stable repository
body:
<!--
If you are reporting a new issue, make sure that we do not have any duplicates
already open. You can ensure this by searching the issue list for this
repository. If there is a duplicate, please close your issue and add a comment
to the existing issue instead.

If you suspect your issue is a bug, please edit your issue description to
include the BUG REPORT INFORMATION shown below. If you fail to provide this
information within 7 days, we cannot debug your issue and will close it. We
will, however, reopen it if you later provide the information.

---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST
-->

**Description**

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

We're on ubuntu 20.04 and our CI infrastructure still relies on the kubic:stable repository. I understand that you decided to move away from maintaining deb packages there and that you suggest to use a newer ubuntu version etc.

But did you intentionally remove buildah from this repo or was it an accident? podman and other packages are still there, versions are unchanged. 

Timestamps suggest that there was a change and also some builds show errors.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. visit kubic stable repo: https://build.opensuse.org/project/show/devel:kubic:libcontainers:stable
2. there is no buildah package

comments.body: Hi @towe75 , Thanks for creating the issue. I think @lsm5 would be the correct person to answer this. @lsm5 Could you PTAL.

Hi @towe75 the kubic packages were causing more trouble than solving, and they were not being maintained anymore. That's why they were deleted. I have also removed podman and skopeo from there now. The only packages that are still there are cri-o and its dependencies.

I'd recommend building buildah from source or using it via the [container image](https://quay.io/organization/buildah) .
